ocr.verbatim:
MR No : 216168
Request No. : 190093765
Patient Name : 
Report Date : 17/03/2026

Date of exam: 17/03/2026
USG ABDOMEN & PELVIS.

LIVER : Increase echogenicity suggestive of grade I fatty changes of liver.
: No focal lesion.
: Intrahepatic biliary radicles are not dilated.
: Portal vein - 10mm (normal).

GALL BLADDER : Measures 9.0 x 4.4 cm with normal bile echotexture.
: Wall thickness - 2mm (normal)
: Calculus of 32 x 12mm is seen as echogenic focus with posterior acoustic shadowing.

CBD : 3mm (normal)

PANCREAS : Size shape and echotexture appears normal with normal pancreatic duct.

SPLEEN : Normal size with homogenous parenchyma.

KIDNEYS: 
: Right : Measures 9.6cm x 3.3cm.
: Left : Measures 9.5cm x 3.6cm.
: Size of kidneys are within normal limits with normal cortical echogenicity.
: Corticomedullary differentiation is well delineated.
: No evidence of calculus or hydronephrotic changes.
: Suprarenal area appears normal.

URINARY BLADDER : Moderately filled. Normal wall thickness.
: No evidence of calculus or intraluminal mass lesion.
: Measures 10.4cm x 5.3cm.

UTERUS : 
: Bulky & normal echogenicity. Two nebothian cysts of 7 to 8mm.

RIGHT OVARY
LEFT OVARY
: Measures 2.0cm x 1.6cm.
: Measures 2.0cm x 1.2cm.
: Both ovaries are of normal size and echotexture.
: Rest of lower abdomen and pelvis reveals normal gas filled bowel loops.
: No significant lymphadenopathy or ascites seen.

IMPRESSION - : Grade I fatty changes of liver.
: Cholelithiasis.

Sr.Consultant Radiologist

records.summary: This is a typed ultrasound report of the abdomen and pelvis, dated March 17, 2026, for a 44-year-old female patient. The report details findings including grade I fatty changes of the liver, a gallstone (32 x 12mm) in the gallbladder, normal-sized kidneys, and two small ovarian cysts. The impression is grade I fatty liver and cholelithiasis.
key_observations: Grade I fatty changes of the liver
Normal pancreas, spleen, and urinary bladder

records.summary: This is a set of ultrasound images showing the right and left kidneys. The images are diagnostic in nature, taken before any procedure, and include measurements and annotations for each kidney.
key_observations: Images show both right and left kidneys with annotations.
Measurements are visible for each kidney (e.g., 3.82 cm, 3.33 cm).
Ultrasound machine interface is visible with patient and scan parameters.
